comments.author: @TruePlanetOffical
comments.text:
If you love wild American landscapes & scenic documentaries, don't miss:
APPALACHIA: The REAL Hidden Wonders of the Oldest Mountains on Earth | 4K Travel Documentary
https://youtu.be/nVPgcrZqVXQ

comments.author: @Bigizzyrc
comments.text: Born and raised in Ruleville Ms. Sunflower County

replies.author: @TruePlanetOffical
replies.text: Thanks for watching! Ruleville and Sunflower County have such a rich history, and it's always special to hear from someone who calls it home. Thanks for sharing! If you enjoy exploring places like this, please like, subscribe, and share the channel—it really helps us keep these documentaries coming.

comments.author: @user-jl5rx9yx2p
comments.text: None of these pictures except the cotton pickers running and some old rotten houses are pictures of the Mississippi Delta. I live in Mississippi right on the river in Greenville, majority of these pictures are closer to Memphis.  Now the winterville mounds are just a few miles north of my house and I live on the river in town, but those old antebellum homes they keep slipping in while talking about the mounds are not even from here except for the Belmont, the square 2 story with double front porches.

replies.author: @TruePlanetOffical
replies.text: That spirit runs deep. 🇺🇸🫡 Thanks for watching—if this video means something to you, share it with someone who’d feel the same.

replies.author: @TruePlanetOffical
replies.text: Nothing beats hitting the road and seeing where it takes you. Where’s your dream road trip destination?

comments.author: @EpicAdventureWorld
comments.text: People talk about “real America” all the time, but maybe the Mississippi Delta shows the part nobody wants to admit exists.

replies.author: @TruePlanetOffical
replies.text: The Delta holds a side of America that’s often overlooked—beautiful, complicated, and deeply real. What do you think people misunderstand most about it?

replies.author: @TruePlanetOffical
replies.text: The blues really does feel like the heartbeat behind so much American music. What blues artist or song always takes you back?

replies.author: @TruePlanetOffical
replies.text: That’s beautiful—there’s nothing quite like being born in a place you’ll always call home. What’s your favorite memory from growing up there? ❤️

comments.author: @EpicEarthYTB
comments.text: A beautiful exploration of the soul of America! It’s fascinating to learn how the Mississippi Delta laid the groundwork for modern music. Truly an incredible story of resilience and culture.

replies.author: @TruePlanetOffical
replies.text: Thank you so much! The Mississippi Delta has such a powerful story behind it—its music, people, and history have shaped so much of America. Is there a Delta artist or song that means a lot to you?

comments.author: @JMustin
comments.text: You have footage in here that isnt within a thousand miles of Mississippi. Good bye loser channel

comments.author: @mysteryvault-h2o
comments.text: I knew the Delta had history, but the way you laid out the blues origins, Robert Johnson’s crossroads, Mound Bayou, and that wild resilience… powerful stuff. The river really carries the soul of America. Thanks for showing the real Delta instead of the usual stereotypes. Subbed!

replies.author: @BillyNix-q4s
replies.text: I can’t speak for blues but Rock And Roll would’ve come along without the delta or blues. The Robert Johnson story about selling his soul is the most ignorant tale ever told. If you’ve ever listened to Johnson’s music he wasn’t endowed with much talent from the Devil or anyone else. Now the delta is a complete wasteland that very few people have any reason to inhabit.

replies.author: @TruePlanetOffical
replies.text: Hey, thanks so much—that really means a lot! I’m glad the real story of the Delta hit home for you. The blues and that unbreakable spirit out there never get old. Have you ever been to the Delta yourself, or is there a blues artist who means the most to you? Welcome aboard!
